StatusNote: The 2021-22 season now includes all four confirmed high-school meets from the archived schedule, from the March 2 Savannah Country Day home meet through the April 22-23 GISA state championships.
RecapParagraphs: The 2021-22 season now gives the track archive another full four-meet year, beginning with two Savannah Country Day home meets and closing with the GISA region and state championship meets. Even in a compact schedule, the results show a team built around sprint points, a few dependable middle-distance efforts, and girls throws production that carried into championship season.

Anthony Kusilka supplied the biggest postseason breakthrough by winning the boys 400 at the Region 2AAA championships and then returning to score again with a fourth-place finish in the state final. Andrew Futch added speed across the spring with scoring runs in the short sprints, a region bronze in the 100, and state-meet appearances in the 100 and long jump.

On the girls side, Morgan Rule anchored the throws group by scoring in shot put at both region and state, while Gray Holmes reached the state meet in the 800 after a third-place region finish. The season page also preserves the boys 4x100 and 4x400 relay groups from both championship meets, giving the 2021-22 archive a clearer picture of the team beyond individual entries.
HighlightNotes: Anthony Kusilka won the boys 400 at the 2022 GISA Region 2AAA meet in 54.09 and followed it with a 53.29 for 4th at the GISA state championships.
Morgan Rule scored in girls shot put at both championship meets, placing 4th at region and 7th at state.
Gray Holmes advanced from a 3rd-place finish in the girls 800 at region to a state-meet berth in the same event.
The boys 4x100 and 4x400 relays both reached the state meet after placing 2nd and 4th, respectively, at the region championships.

StatusNote: The 2022-23 season now includes four verified meets, from the two Savannah Country Day regular-season meets through the Region 2AAA and GIAA state championship meets.
RecapParagraphs: The 2022-23 season now gives the track archive a full four-meet year to work from, starting with two Savannah Country Day regular-season meets and ending with region and state championship competition. Even in a short schedule, the loaded results show a balanced team with sprint speed, developing distance depth, and a girls throws group that scored when the meets mattered most.

Grace Dare emerged as one of the season's clearest postseason performers by placing second in both the girls 1600 and 3200 at the Region 2AAA championships before moving on to the state meet in those same distance events. On the boys side, Andrew Futch supplied the biggest sprint points, finishing runner-up in both the 100 and 200 at region and then advancing to state in the short sprints and long jump.

The season page also captures a broad supporting cast around those headline results. Morgan Rule scored in the girls shot put at region and qualified for state, the girls 4x800 relay reached both region and state, and Nick Brown, Ian Given, Ryder Small, Tripp Jackson, and the young throws group all helped shape the identity of the team.
HighlightNotes: Grace Dare placed 2nd in both the girls 1600 and 3200 at the 2023 Region 2AAA championships, then qualified on to the GIAA state meet in both events.
Andrew Futch finished 2nd in both the boys 100 and 200 at region and later competed at state in the 100, 200, and long jump.
Morgan Rule scored with a 3rd-place finish in girls shot put at region and went on to place 11th at the state meet.
The girls 4x800 relay reached both region and state, with the region lineup of Maren Wrieden, Sydney Mims, Gray Holmes, and Grace Dare posted in the public results.

StatusNote: The 2023-24 season currently includes the two postseason meets St. Andrew's contested in the public archive: the April 17, 2024 GIAA Region 2AAA Championships and the April 25-27, 2024 GIAA AAA State Meet.
RecapParagraphs: The 2023-24 track season currently appears in the public archive as a short postseason run, with St. Andrew's results loaded from the Region 2AAA championships and the GIAA AAA state meet. Even with just those two meets, the file already shows the shape of the team clearly: throws points, distance entries, and sprint-relay depth on the boys side.

Tripp Jackson delivered the headline region result by winning the boys shot put and placing fourth in discus, while Nick Brown and Ryder Small gave the team scoring and state-level presence in the distance races. Ellie Waldron also scored in the girls shot put at region before advancing to the state meet.

Because the loaded season is concentrated in championship competition, the 2023-24 page reads as a postseason snapshot rather than a full week-to-week schedule. It still adds an important earlier chapter to the track archive and pushes the section back one more school year.
HighlightNotes: Tripp Jackson won the boys shot put at the Region 2AAA championships with a 43-1.50 and also placed 4th in discus.
Nick Brown placed 2nd in the boys 3200 at region and returned to the GIAA AAA state meet in the same event.
Ellie Waldron scored in the girls shot put at region and later competed at state.
The boys 4x100, 4x400, and 4x800 relays all appear in the region results, with the 4x100 relay placing 4th.

StatusNote: The 2024-25 season currently includes five verified St. Andrew's meets, ending with the April 16, 2025 region championships and the May 1-3, 2025 state championships.
StateMeetLocation: Strong Rock Christian School, Locust Grove
RecapParagraphs: The 2024-25 season is the first St. Andrew's track year currently loaded meet-by-meet in the site, and it already shows the identity of the program clearly: distance depth, developing relay chemistry, and throws groups that kept adding points as the spring moved along.

Grace Dare opened the year with a runner-up finish in the girls 800, Lily McAtee advanced to the state meet in the 200, and Ellie Waldron gave the girls side a steady presence in the throws. On the boys side, Miles Cummings kept improving in the shot put, and the 4x800 relay turned into one of the season's clearest progression stories.

By postseason, the archive shows St. Andrew's athletes competing in distance races, sprints, jumps, throws, and relays at both region and state. That gives the section a strong first season page and a useful base for future event-group and championship-history additions.
HighlightNotes: Grace Dare placed 2nd in the girls 800 meters with a 2:34.51 at Savannah Country Day School Varsity Meet #2.
Miles Cummings closed the season with a 42-6.25 shot put at the GIAA state meet.
The boys 4x800 relay improved from 9:37.88 at the first loaded meet to 9:10.25 at state.
State-meet entries are loaded for Lily McAtee, Ryder Small, Miles Cummings, Ellie Waldron, Grace Dare, and the boys 4x800 relay.

StatusNote: As of April 20, 2026, the season meet list below has been confirmed from the current team schedule. The April 15, 2026 Savannah Country Day School Varsity Meet #6 has been intentionally omitted because St. Andrew's did not participate.
StateMeetLocation: Strong Rock Christian School, Locust Grove
RecapParagraphs: The 2025-26 season file is still growing, but the first four completed meets already show more event coverage across both the girls and boys groups. The section now has enough data to track weekly progress instead of only listing a schedule.

Aubri Rutledge has supplied the headline sprint marks so far on the girls side, while Janiyah Johnson has anchored the throws with strong discus and shot put performances. The distance group has also stayed active every week, with Lane Mandel and Nick Brown providing the most visible middle-distance and distance marks in the current file.

On the boys side, the 4x100 relay is already down to 45.70, Luke Nelson has paired sprint speed with a strong discus mark, and Josiah Bowers has contributed in both the high jump and triple jump. With region and state still ahead on the calendar, this page is designed to keep filling in as postseason results are added.
HighlightNotes: Aubri Rutledge has posted the team's best girls sprint marks so far with a 13.32 in the 100 and a 27.09 in the 200.
The boys 4x100 relay has already reached 45.70 through the first four completed meets.
Lane Mandel's 13:08.48 in the 3200 and Nick Brown's 5:11.95 in the 1600 lead the loaded distance results.
Region and state dates are already on the timeline, so the meet-by-meet archive can keep growing through postseason.
